title: CONDENSATION OF ALDEHYDES WITH MALONIC ACID. PART XVIII. CONDENSATION OF 5-NITRO-2-HYDROXYBENZALDEHYDE
author: KANTILAL C. PANDYA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: 5-Nitro-2-hydroxybenzaldehyde (5-nitrosalicyladehyde) is condensed with malonic acid. It does not enter into condensation in the absence of any condensing reagent or even in the presence of glacial acetic acid. In the presence of pyridine, piperidine or a mixture of the two, it gives 5-nitro-2-hydroxy- benzylidene-malonic (5-nitrosalicylidene-malonic) acid, the highest yield, so far obtained, being 84% in five hours' heating at 80° : as salicylaldehyde has always given much lower yields, the increase could be due only to the influence of the nitro group in 5 position. The corresponding - mono-acid has, however, not been obtained yet from either the aldehyde or the dibasic acid.
steps_raw: 5-Nitro-2-hydroxybenzaldehyde (5-nitrosalicyladehyde) is condensed with malonic acid. It does not enter into condensation in the absence of any condensing reagent or even in the presence of glacial acetic acid. In the presence of pyridine, piperidine or a mixture of the two, it gives 5-nitro-2-hydroxy- benzylidene-malonic (5-nitrosalicylidene-malonic) acid, the highest yield, so far obtained, being 84% in five hours' heating at 80° : as salicylaldehyde has always given much lower yields, the increase could be due only to the influence of the nitro group in 5 position. The corresponding - mono-acid has, however, not been obtained yet from either the aldehyde or the dibasic acid.
